acceptance_targets: [CH05_PROSE_001_ELLIPTICAL_CLIPPING] Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:57; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:71; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:119; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:121; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:133; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:139; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:209; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:215; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:217; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:219 reads as complete prose sentences rather than dropped-subject or dropped-verb fragments, while each cited beat remains legible on the page. Fail if the revised passage still relies on fragment bursts like "Not decision," "The warmth," "Pale. Cold.", or "Eleven years" for emphasis.

[CH05_PROSE_002_TEMPLATE_RECURRENCE] Pass if a cold read of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:61; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:63; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:81; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:95; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:119; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:139; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:175 still yields the same narrative information, but the cited beats no longer repeat the same correction-template emphasis from paragraph to paragraph. Fail if multiple cited paragraphs still hinge on another "not X/but Y" correction or "the specific/the way" qualifier where direct rendering would do the work.
[R-09] Ch 5 em-dash density is at or below 15% of prose lines. No paragraph contains more than one em-dash pair.
[R-12] Ch 5 'He'-initial sentences do not exceed 7% of total sentences. No three consecutive sentences begin with the same pronoun.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:57; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:71; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:119; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:121; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:133; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:139; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:209; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:215; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:217; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:219 reads as complete prose sentences rather than dropped-subject or dropped-verb fragments, while each cited beat remains legible on the page. Fail if the revised passage still relies on fragment bursts like "Not decision," "The warmth," "Pale. Cold.", or "Eleven years" for emphasis.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly drops into clipped noun-phrase fragments to force intensity: "Not decision. Not thought.", "The texture of the stone. The warmth.", "Pale. Cold.", "Eleven years." The constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration, and the recurrence here makes the chapter sound workshopped-thriller rather than fully inhabited.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:57; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:71; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:119; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:121; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:133; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:139; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:209; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:215; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:217; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:219, convert the stand-alone fragments into grammatically complete narration or fold them into adjacent sentences. Preserve the concrete beat in each spot: instinct taking over, the altered fighting stance, mechanical vomiting, Derry's concealment, wound assessment, bandaging, Aldric's failed comfort, the cold campsite atmosphere, the tactile memory of the stab, and Maren's final realization.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold read of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:61; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:63; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:81; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:95; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:119; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:139; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:175 still yields the same narrative information, but the cited beats no longer repeat the same correction-template emphasis from paragraph to paragraph. Fail if multiple cited paragraphs still hinge on another "not X/but Y" correction or "the specific/the way" qualifier where direct rendering would do the work.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps reaching for emphasis through the same marked scaffolds: correction moves ("not X but Y" / "Not X — Y"), "the specific..." qualifiers, and "the way..." comparison frames. Because these arrive in the opening, fight, aftermath, and Aldric/Vess beats alike, vivid material starts sounding pre-shaped by one rhetorical mold instead of scene-specific prose pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the sentence machinery at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:61; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:63; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:81; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:95; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:119; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:139; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:175. Keep the strongest contrast where it genuinely sharpens perception, but rebuild the rest through direct image, action, sound, or a different sentence shape so the chapter does not keep leaning on the same self-correcting hinge.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 em-dash density is at or below 15% of prose lines. No paragraph contains more than one em-dash pair.
findings.evidence: Ch 5 has 58 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 109 total prose lines = 53.2% density. Manuscript-wide: 979/2406 = 40.7%. Threshold: 10%. The manuscript exceeds the threshold by a factor of 4. Ch 5 is the densest chapter.
findings.problem: Em-dash density in Ch 5 is 53.2% — more than half of all prose lines contain at least one em-dash. The manuscript-wide density of 40.7% is 4x the 10% threshold. Em-dashes are used for parenthetical asides, interrupted speech, mid-sentence pivots, and appositives, creating a rhythmic monotony where nearly every long sentence contains the same punctuation gesture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 5 from 58 to no more than 11 (targeting 10% density). Convert parenthetical em-dashes to commas, semicolons, or separate sentences. Reserve em-dashes for genuine interruptions and dramatic pivots. This chapter contains the first kill — the interrupted rhythm should be the exception, not the baseline.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 'He'-initial sentences do not exceed 7% of total sentences. No three consecutive sentences begin with the same pronoun.
findings.evidence: Ch 5 has 54 sentences beginning with 'He' out of 310 total sentences = 17.4%. Manuscript-wide: 669/6236 = 10.7%. Threshold: 7% for any single pronoun. Ch 5 is the densest chapter for 'He' openers.
findings.problem: 17.4% of sentences in Ch 5 begin with 'He,' creating a rhythmic monotony in a chapter that should have maximum kinetic variety (first combat, first kill). The pronoun-opener density is 2.5x the 7% threshold. When nearly one in five sentences starts with the same word, the prose loses the ability to signal shifts in attention or agency.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'He' openers in Ch 5 from 54 to no more than 22 (targeting 7%). Vary sentence openings with action leads ('The sword came down'), sensory leads ('Something cracked'), participial phrases, or dialogue. In combat sequences, begin sentences with what Maren perceives rather than what he does.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
